summary: nolimit-x is an obfuscated bulk SMS/email phishing toolkit
details: nolimit-x ships as a heavily obfuscated phishing/spam toolkit advertised as an 'Advanced email sender' for 'red team operations.' All 45 source files under.ad/ are obfuscated with javascript-obfuscator (RC4 string arrays, self-defending wrappers, control-flow flattening), including the CLI entry point x0.js. After deobfuscation the package implements: (1) bulk SMS phishing with template payloads such as 'Hi {{NAME}}, your verification code is {{RANDOM(6)}}. Do not share this code.' delivered via SMTP-to-SMS gateways with rotation/MX-bypass; (2) a 'contacts' subcommand that uses device-code OAuth against Microsoft Graph and Google to enumerate contact lists from already-compromised mailboxes (tokens loaded from smtps.txt) and writes them to contacts/<account>/<provider>.txt as a victim-list builder; (3) a 'web inject' panel for hijacking webmail sessions. The combination of phishing-template payloads, mailbox contact harvesters, and full-package obfuscation is consistent with attacker tooling intended to victimize third parties at scale. While there is no install-time RCE or installer-data exfiltration (the CLI runs only when explicitly invoked), the package itself is malware-as-a-product hosted on npm and should not be available for installation.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
